entries.name: ValidatorTimelock
entries.description: Intermediary contract between the *Validators* and the central diamond contract that delays block execution (ie withdrawals and other L2 --> L1 messages) by 3h.

receivedPermissions.description: commit, prove, execute, revert batches directly in the main Diamond contract. This role is typically held by a proxying ValidatorTimelock.

administrate operator roles for this chain in the ValidatorTimelock, manage fees, apply predefined upgrades, manage censorship through a TransactionFilterer, set DA mode, migrate the chain to whitelisted settlement layers (Chain Admin role).

entries.name: DualVerifier
entries.description: A router contract for verifiers. Routes verification requests to eth:0xE3743181a4b0A0C1260826105c6BBA4b6e18D79d or eth:0xB3f4396C2040e502d0556Cbb16C0B22fE777A026 depending on the supplied proof type.

verificationKeyHash.description: Verification key hash for the PLONK verifier ONLY (backwards compatibility).

receivedPermissions.description: initiate batch execution on L1 if approved by enough Validator Multisig members.

entries.name: L1VerifierPlonk
entries.description: Verifies a zk-SNARK proof using an implementation of the PlonK proof system.

verificationKeyHash.description: keccak256 hash of the verification keys.

entries.name: EraMultisigValidator
entries.description: A multisig wrapper around `ValidatorTimelock` that requires a threshold of approvals before batch execution can proceed, provides additional security through 2FA.

receivedPermissions.description: Permissioned to call the functions to commit, prove, execute and revert L2 batches through the ValidatorTimelock in the main Diamond contract.

entries.name: ZeroNetworkZkEvmAdmin
entries.description: A governance proxy that lets eth:0x2e5BE1479cF661eeD9F526b7926eA87F6A5dD6a9 act through it.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: administrate operator roles for this chain in the ValidatorTimelock, manage fees, apply predefined upgrades, manage censorship through a TransactionFilterer, set DA mode, migrate the chain to whitelisted settlement layers (Chain Admin role).

upgradeTimestamps.description: Timestamps for new protocol version upgrades can be registered here (NOT enforced)

receivedPermissions.description: initiate batch execution on L1 if approved by enough Validator Multisig members.

entries.name: Diamond
entries.description: The main contract defining the Layer 2. Operator actions like commiting blocks, providing ZK proofs and executing batches ultimately target this contract which then processes transactions. During batch execution it processes L1 --> L2 and L2 --> L1 transactions.

getTransactionFilterer.description: This contract must expose the ITransactionFilterer interface (see Mailbox facet) and is used for censoring transactions pushed from L1 to L2.

IsPorterAvailableStatus.description: zkPorter is a volition-like contruction and changes the zk proof input requirements.

isPermanentRollup.description: true means that the DA mode cannot be changed to Validium in the future. compliant DAValidator pairs for the permanent rollup mode are defined/enforced by the RollupDAManager contract.

getProtocolVersion.description: Protocol version, increments with each protocol upgrade.

getVerifierParams.description: Verifier parameters used for proving batches.

arg.1: . isPermanentRollup was set to true in this contract which prevents changing the DA mode to Validium in the future.

entries.name: L1VerifierFflonk
entries.description: Verifies a zk-SNARK proof using an implementation of the fflonk proof system.

verificationKeyHash.description: keccak256 hash of the verification keys.
